sections.paragraph:
παρακαθίζω (Pla. et al., mostly in the mid., as Jos., Ant. 19, 264) in our lit. only in act. sit down beside τινί someone (Diod S 23, 9, 5; Plut., Mar. 415 [17, 3], Cleom. 823 [37, 16], Mor. 58d; Job 2:13; TestSol 17:2 P [π. ἐμαυτόν]; TestJob) Hv 5:2. πρὸς τοὺς πόδας τινός Lk 10:39 v.l.—DELG s.v. ἕζομαι.

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

act. act. = active

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Cleom Cleom , II a.d.—List 5

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

TestJob TestJob = Testament of Job—List 2

Hv Hv = Visions

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 764
